description_long: This is a short section of a wooden fence, featuring three vertical posts with beveled tops and two horizontal rails connecting them. The wood is rendered with a rich brown color and visible grain patterns, giving it a natural and slightly stylized appearance. The vertical posts are evenly spaced and have a slightly wider profile than the rails, providing structural stability. The surface finish appears smooth with a subtle sheen, suggesting a polished or varnished wood. There are no visible fasteners or decorative elements beyond the wood grain texture. This type of fence is commonly used in urban parks, gardens, or as a boundary marker in residential areas.
description: A short, brown wooden fence with three vertical posts and two horizontal rails, featuring visible wood grain and beveled tops.
description_view_0: Front view showing three vertical posts with beveled tops and two horizontal rails, highlighting the wood grain texture.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin profile of the fence, with the posts and rails aligned in a flat plane.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the same three posts and two rails with wood grain details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and alignment of the fence components.
